terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster's "adversarial" — two sides set up to oppose each other on purpose, like a court's defense vs. prosecution. Applied here: one checker's job is to oppose the work, not agree with it. Is NOT: checking your own work · a quick "looks fine" · hostility for its own sake Example: the critique loop's checker attacks the work before it ships; checker is never the same system as the maker
terms.is_not: checking your own work · a quick "looks fine" · hostility for its own sake Example: the critique loop's checker attacks the work before it ships; checker is never the same system as the maker
terms.example: the critique loop's checker attacks the work before it ships; checker is never the same system as the maker
terms.public_phrasing: "checked by a separate reviewer whose job is to look for weaknesses before release"

terms.meaning: IBM's "agentic AI" — software that plans and acts on its own, not just answers when asked — plus FinOps "cost governance" — real budgets and limits on spend, not just a bill you read after. Together: limits placed on AI that acts by itself, so it can't spend unchecked. Is NOT: a spending report you read after the money's gone · "we use AI responsibly" with no budget attached · a dashboard with no power to stop anything Example: Tier 1 "AI Spend Leak Audit" finds where autonomous AI spend has no cap, before selling any enforcement
terms.is_not: a spending report you read after the money's gone · "we use AI responsibly" with no budget attached · a dashboard with no power to stop anything Example: Tier 1 "AI Spend Leak Audit" finds where autonomous AI spend has no cap, before selling any enforcement
terms.example: Tier 1 "AI Spend Leak Audit" finds where autonomous AI spend has no cap, before selling any enforcement

terms.meaning: standard software-pipeline role — collects multiple checkers' outputs and combines them into one verdict. Is NOT: a single checker · a vote without a rule for ties Example: IDE lane pipeline combines critic + verifier outputs before a merge decision
terms.is_not: a single checker · a vote without a rule for ties Example: IDE lane pipeline combines critic + verifier outputs before a merge decision
terms.example: IDE lane pipeline combines critic + verifier outputs before a merge decision
terms.public_phrasing: "the step that combines the checks into one result"

terms.meaning: standard security/ops term — noticing when something departs from its normal pattern. Is NOT: a person eyeballing a dashboard · a rule that only checks one fixed threshold Example: Line Engine drops a line a gear, or to FROZEN, the moment it detects anomaly
terms.is_not: a person eyeballing a dashboard · a rule that only checks one fixed threshold Example: Line Engine drops a line a gear, or to FROZEN, the moment it detects anomaly
terms.example: Line Engine drops a line a gear, or to FROZEN, the moment it detects anomaly

terms.meaning: built on the established term "security theater" (measures that look protective but do nothing) — the opposite discipline: never let a system perform safety without proving it. Is NOT: skepticism for its own sake · disbelieving every receipt Example: negative-proof doctrine — a gate isn't trusted until it's shown rejecting a bad input
terms.is_not: skepticism for its own sake · disbelieving every receipt Example: negative-proof doctrine — a gate isn't trusted until it's shown rejecting a bad input
terms.example: negative-proof doctrine — a gate isn't trusted until it's shown rejecting a bad input
terms.public_phrasing: "we prove safety, not perform it" Related: child pattern under Pattern Factory.

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster — one who designs and reviews structure. Here: the role that designs, reviews, and cuts unneeded work, rather than writing features. Is NOT: the person who ships code · the founder Example: Architect locks a spec before a worker builds it
terms.is_not: the person who ships code · the founder Example: Architect locks a spec before a worker builds it
terms.example: Architect locks a spec before a worker builds it

terms.meaning: standard software term ("build artifact") — a saved, versioned file a process produces. Is NOT: chat scratch · an unsaved draft Example: a signed release file in the library
terms.is_not: chat scratch · an unsaved draft Example: a signed release file in the library
terms.example: a signed release file in the library

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "audit" (an official examination) + "loop" (a repeating cycle) — a recurring, scheduled examination. Is NOT: a one-time look · an internal opinion with no schedule Example: `P8-MACHINE-LOOPS/audit-loop.md`, canon §5
terms.is_not: a one-time look · an internal opinion with no schedule Example: `P8-MACHINE-LOOPS/audit-loop.md`, canon §5

terms.meaning: a repair pattern that watches system health continuously and fixes known-safe breakages automatically, the way a hospital's standing protocols treat common conditions without waiting for a doctor's fresh judgment each time. Is NOT: the same thing as Self-heal (that's the Line Engine's own bounded repair loop) · a fix for anything unknown or unsafe Example: `P9-PATTERN-FACTORY/auto-heal-hospital.md`
terms.is_not: the same thing as Self-heal (that's the Line Engine's own bounded repair loop) · a fix for anything unknown or unsafe Example: `P9-PATTERN-FACTORY/auto-heal-hospital.md`
terms.public_phrasing: "automatic repair of known, safe issues" Related: child pattern under Pattern Factory, distinct from Self-heal (per founder ruling).  ---
terms.related: child pattern under Pattern Factory, distinct from Self-heal (per founder ruling).  ---

terms.meaning: AI-industry standard term — a pretrained model with no added "assistant" instruction-tuning or house style baked in. Is NOT: a chat-tuned assistant model · "any small model" Example: the brain runs on a base model specifically so it has no competing definitions of "done" or "verified"
terms.is_not: a chat-tuned assistant model · "any small model" Example: the brain runs on a base model specifically so it has no competing definitions of "done" or "verified"
terms.example: the brain runs on a base model specifically so it has no competing definitions of "done" or "verified"

terms.meaning: a base model has no pre-baked definitions of words like "done" or "verified," so our own definitions become its only meaning for those words, instead of competing with a prior meaning. In our system: this is why SSOT and the R1/R2/R6/R7 rules load before anything else — they're not policy sitting beside the model, they're the model's vocabulary. Is NOT: true of assistant-tuned models (Cursor, GPT-assistant) — those already carry competing definitions, which is what causes compliance theater Example: `P7-DOCTRINE/base-model-first-class-language.md` Doctrine source file: `base-model-first-class-language.md` Public-safe rewrite: "we load our own definitions before the model forms any of its own"
terms.in_our_system: this is why SSOT and the R1/R2/R6/R7 rules load before anything else — they're not policy sitting beside the model, they're the model's vocabulary. Is NOT: true of assistant-tuned models (Cursor, GPT-assistant) — those already carry competing definitions, which is what causes compliance theater Example: `P7-DOCTRINE/base-model-first-class-language.md` Doctrine source file: `base-model-first-class-language.md` Public-safe rewrite: "we load our own definitions before the model forms any of its own"
terms.is_not: true of assistant-tuned models (Cursor, GPT-assistant) — those already carry competing definitions, which is what causes compliance theater Example: `P7-DOCTRINE/base-model-first-class-language.md` Doctrine source file: `base-model-first-class-language.md` Public-safe rewrite: "we load our own definitions before the model forms any of its own"
terms.example: `P7-DOCTRINE/base-model-first-class-language.md` Doctrine source file: `base-model-first-class-language.md` Public-safe rewrite: "we load our own definitions before the model forms any of its own"
terms.public_phrasing: "we load our own definitions before the model forms any of its own"
terms.doctrine_source_file: `base-model-first-class-language.md` Public-safe rewrite: "we load our own definitions before the model forms any of its own"
terms.banned_surfaces: customer-facing copy (too technical to be useful publicly)

terms.meaning: from Kubernetes' standard "probe" pattern (liveness/readiness checks) — a check that verifies a system actually behaves correctly, not just that it deployed. Is NOT: a deploy log · an uptime check with no behavior test Example: Line Engine's mandatory pre-promotion behavior probe
terms.is_not: a deploy log · an uptime check with no behavior test Example: Line Engine's mandatory pre-promotion behavior probe
terms.example: Line Engine's mandatory pre-promotion behavior probe

terms.meaning: standard SRE/security term — a cap on how much damage one failure can cause. Is NOT: a general "be careful" guideline · unlimited scope with a warning label Example: Line Engine's per-line blast-radius limits, checked every window
terms.is_not: a general "be careful" guideline · unlimited scope with a warning label Example: Line Engine's per-line blast-radius limits, checked every window
terms.example: Line Engine's per-line blast-radius limits, checked every window
terms.public_phrasing: "a limit on how much damage one failure can cause"

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster — a detailed plan. Is NOT: a vague idea · a finished product Example: Line Engine blueprint registry
terms.is_not: a vague idea · a finished product Example: Line Engine blueprint registry

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "registry" (an official recorded list) applied to blueprints — a governed catalog of approved plans. Is NOT: a folder of random files Example: `P5-LINE-ENGINE/blueprint-registry.md`
terms.is_not: a folder of random files Example: `P5-LINE-ENGINE/blueprint-registry.md`

terms.meaning: our name for a rule-based system that routes and decides using fixed definitions, not guesses. Is NOT: a chat assistant · a language model making its own judgment calls Example: the brain classifies intent and picks a route from locked-definitions, never invents a new one
terms.is_not: a chat assistant · a language model making its own judgment calls Example: the brain classifies intent and picks a route from locked-definitions, never invents a new one
terms.example: the brain classifies intent and picks a route from locked-definitions, never invents a new one
terms.public_phrasing: "a rules-based decision system" — CODE_ALIAS: "Brain" is our product name for it; the plain description above is the real meaning.

terms.meaning: built on the standard industry pattern "X-as-a-Service" (SaaS, PaaS) — the same rules-engine we run internally, offered as a delivered service. Is NOT: a chatbot subscription Example: `P9-PATTERN-FACTORY/brain-as-a-service.md`
terms.is_not: a chatbot subscription Example: `P9-PATTERN-FACTORY/brain-as-a-service.md`
terms.public_phrasing: "operating brain, installed for you" Related: child pattern under Pattern Factory.

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "audit" applied to a brain's rules — an examination of whether its decision rules are sound. Is NOT: an audit of company finances Example: `P9-PATTERN-FACTORY/brain-audit-v1.md`, Tier-1 sellable offer
terms.is_not: an audit of company finances Example: `P9-PATTERN-FACTORY/brain-audit-v1.md`, Tier-1 sellable offer

terms.meaning: standard software-resilience term (the "circuit breaker pattern") — stop calling a failing dependency automatically instead of hammering it. Is NOT: a manual off switch · a retry loop with no stop condition Example: capability router's circuit breaker
terms.is_not: a manual off switch · a retry loop with no stop condition Example: capability router's circuit breaker

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster — a statement asserting something is true. Is NOT: a receipt Example: "Worker said PASS" with no receipt is a claim, not proof
terms.is_not: a receipt Example: "Worker said PASS" with no receipt is a claim, not proof
terms.example: "Worker said PASS" with no receipt is a claim, not proof
terms.public_phrasing: "an unverified statement" (when no receipt backs it)

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "alignment" (agreement in position) applied to business — the contract terms partners actually operate under. Is NOT: a free-access request Example: terminology v1 §5
terms.is_not: a free-access request Example: terminology v1 §5

terms.meaning: a fixed, structured plan format (the Execution Contract) — not the language model — decides what's allowed to run, what output counts as valid, and what it costs. In our system: the model only proposes a plan; it never has authority to execute anything the contract hasn't approved. Is NOT: "the model is in charge" · "we trust the model's judgment at runtime" Example: `P7-DOCTRINE/contracts-run-the-system.md` Doctrine source file: `contracts-run-the-system.md` Public-safe rewrite: "a fixed set of rules decides what runs — not the AI itself"
terms.in_our_system: the model only proposes a plan; it never has authority to execute anything the contract hasn't approved. Is NOT: "the model is in charge" · "we trust the model's judgment at runtime" Example: `P7-DOCTRINE/contracts-run-the-system.md` Doctrine source file: `contracts-run-the-system.md` Public-safe rewrite: "a fixed set of rules decides what runs — not the AI itself"
terms.is_not: "the model is in charge" · "we trust the model's judgment at runtime" Example: `P7-DOCTRINE/contracts-run-the-system.md` Doctrine source file: `contracts-run-the-system.md` Public-safe rewrite: "a fixed set of rules decides what runs — not the AI itself"
terms.example: `P7-DOCTRINE/contracts-run-the-system.md` Doctrine source file: `contracts-run-the-system.md` Public-safe rewrite: "a fixed set of rules decides what runs — not the AI itself"
terms.public_phrasing: "a fixed set of rules decides what runs — not the AI itself"
terms.doctrine_source_file: `contracts-run-the-system.md` Public-safe rewrite: "a fixed set of rules decides what runs — not the AI itself"
terms.banned_surfaces: casual marketing copy (too technical to land as a soundbite)

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "cap" — a fixed upper limit. All work stops once it's hit. Is NOT: a soft budget guideline Example: terminology v1 §2
terms.is_not: a soft budget guideline Example: terminology v1 §2

terms.meaning: standard agentic-AI pipeline role — a component whose only job is to find problems in another component's output. Is NOT: the producer reviewing itself Example: `adversarial_critic_receipt_v1.py`
terms.is_not: the producer reviewing itself Example: `adversarial_critic_receipt_v1.py`

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "critique" (a critical evaluation) + "loop" — a recurring, scheduled critical evaluation. Is NOT: a one-off comment Example: `P8-MACHINE-LOOPS/critique-loop.md`, canon §3
terms.is_not: a one-off comment Example: `P8-MACHINE-LOOPS/critique-loop.md`, canon §3

terms.meaning: the real, live state of the system, checked directly (an HTTP response, a live page, a database row), not what a commit message or log claims. Is NOT: "git push succeeded" · a deploy log Example: CF health check showing 14 live targets vs. 7 in the repo = drift caught by deploy-truth
terms.is_not: "git push succeeded" · a deploy log Example: CF health check showing 14 live targets vs. 7 in the repo = drift caught by deploy-truth
terms.example: CF health check showing 14 live targets vs. 7 in the repo = drift caught by deploy-truth
terms.public_phrasing: "verified live status, not just a deploy log"

terms.meaning: standard startup/enterprise-sales term — an early customer who tests a product in exchange for shaping it, before it's a finished sale. Is NOT: a fully committed paying customer Example: terminology v1 §5
terms.is_not: a fully committed paying customer Example: terminology v1 §5

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster — relating to identifying a problem, not fixing or blocking it. Is NOT: "firewall deployed" (that's enforcement) unless a customer receipt proves it Example: Tier 1 "AI Spend Leak Audit" is diagnostic; Tier 3 "Firewall Pilot" is enforcement
terms.is_not: "firewall deployed" (that's enforcement) unless a customer receipt proves it Example: Tier 1 "AI Spend Leak Audit" is diagnostic; Tier 3 "Firewall Pilot" is enforcement
terms.example: Tier 1 "AI Spend Leak Audit" is diagnostic; Tier 3 "Firewall Pilot" is enforcement

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster — a settled principle or rule. Here: a locked operating rule not re-argued each session. Is NOT: a chat opinion · a draft idea Example: `P7-DOCTRINE/`
terms.is_not: a chat opinion · a draft idea Example: `P7-DOCTRINE/`

terms.meaning: standard DevOps term "configuration drift" — the live system no longer matching its approved definition. Is NOT: "we should improve this someday" Example: a cron schedule that no longer matches the approved registry file
terms.is_not: "we should improve this someday" Example: a cron schedule that no longer matches the approved registry file
terms.example: a cron schedule that no longer matches the approved registry file
terms.public_phrasing: "mismatch between what's approved and what's actually running"  ---

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster — to compel compliance, actively. Is NOT: describing a policy without applying it Example: pairs with Diagnostic — enforcement requires proof of live installation
terms.is_not: describing a policy without applying it Example: pairs with Diagnostic — enforcement requires proof of live installation
terms.example: pairs with Diagnostic — enforcement requires proof of live installation

terms.meaning: standard incident-management term — a fixed sequence of steps for handling a problem, in order. Is NOT: "use the biggest model when unsure" Example: terminology v1 §2
terms.is_not: "use the biggest model when unsure" Example: terminology v1 §2

terms.meaning: standard software/legal term (a binding, structured agreement on what may run) — the fixed schema that decides what a plan is allowed to do before it runs. Is NOT: a suggestion the model can reinterpret Example: `P5-LINE-ENGINE/execution-contract-as-brain.md`
terms.is_not: a suggestion the model can reinterpret Example: `P5-LINE-ENGINE/execution-contract-as-brain.md`
terms.public_phrasing: "a fixed rulebook that decides what's allowed to run"  ---

terms.meaning: standard security-engineering term — when a check fails, the system stops, rather than continuing. Is NOT: warn-and-continue Example: terminology v1 §2
terms.is_not: warn-and-continue Example: terminology v1 §2

terms.meaning: fixed labels in the workflow census: FIX = repair a loop that serves a real user; RETIRE = shut down a loop with no real user. Is NOT: a judgment call made loosely Example: "workflow_audit FIX; inbox RETIRE"
terms.is_not: a judgment call made loosely Example: "workflow_audit FIX; inbox RETIRE"

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster — the person who starts and holds ultimate authority over an organization. Here: Sina, who approves only irreversible actions. Is NOT: a manager · an approver of everyday changes Example: terminology v1 §3
terms.is_not: a manager · an approver of everyday changes Example: terminology v1 §3

terms.meaning: our own test applied to any proposed rule or process — does it actually serve what the founder is trying to do, or just add process for its own sake. Is NOT: a rubber-stamp · a vote Example: `P7-DOCTRINE/founder-intent-filter.md`
terms.is_not: a rubber-stamp · a vote Example: `P7-DOCTRINE/founder-intent-filter.md`

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "govern" (to control by rule) — a process the system can stop, measure, and override without asking the agent running it. Is NOT: "we use AI responsibly" with no actual stop mechanism Example: a loop with a cost cap, kill switch, and a receipt on every run
terms.is_not: "we use AI responsibly" with no actual stop mechanism Example: a loop with a cost cap, kill switch, and a receipt on every run
terms.example: a loop with a cost cap, kill switch, and a receipt on every run
terms.public_phrasing: "governed" only if it's actually stoppable and measured — otherwise, downgrade the claim

terms.meaning: a real, inspectable, receipt-backed setup a partner can check directly, instead of a claimed customer list. Is NOT: a logo wall · an unprovable "500+ clients" claim Example: terminology v1 §5
terms.is_not: a logo wall · an unprovable "500+ clients" claim Example: terminology v1 §5

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster — a central point that other things connect to. Here: the founder's control plane — spawns, pauses, and kills lines; approves only irreversible actions. Is NOT: a place that executes work itself Example: `P5-LINE-ENGINE/LINE_ENGINE_ARCHITECTURE_v0.1.md` §2
terms.is_not: a place that executes work itself Example: `P5-LINE-ENGINE/LINE_ENGINE_ARCHITECTURE_v0.1.md` §2

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "immutable" (unchangeable) + "floor" (a lower limit) — the safety rules (verifier, kill-switch, gates) that a self-improving system is never allowed to weaken from the inside. Is NOT: a suggestion the system can optimize away · a rule the loop can edit if it decides to Example: any change touching a floor path auto-fails and freezes the line — founder-only, out of band
terms.is_not: a suggestion the system can optimize away · a rule the loop can edit if it decides to Example: any change touching a floor path auto-fails and freezes the line — founder-only, out of band
terms.example: any change touching a floor path auto-fails and freezes the line — founder-only, out of band
terms.public_phrasing: "the safety rules the system can never weaken on its own"  ---

terms.meaning: standard computer-science term — the core of a system that everything else runs on top of. Is NOT: a script that runs once Example: terminology v1 §1
terms.is_not: a script that runs once Example: terminology v1 §1

terms.meaning: standard product/pilot-management term — the conditions, written down before a pilot starts, that will stop it if unmet. Is NOT: a judgment made after the fact Example: terminology v1 §2
terms.is_not: a judgment made after the fact Example: terminology v1 §2

terms.meaning: standard safety-engineering term — a mechanism that stops a system immediately. Is NOT: a slow manual shutdown process Example: the substrate's global kill-switch
terms.is_not: a slow manual shutdown process Example: the substrate's global kill-switch

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "lane" (a defined path) — a repo-scoped workstream with its own rules. Is NOT: a vague category · the same thing as "Line" (see Line — different concept, cross-referenced) Example: the IDE Lane work order, scoped to one repo
terms.is_not: a vague category · the same thing as "Line" (see Line — different concept, cross-referenced) Example: the IDE Lane work order, scoped to one repo
terms.example: the IDE Lane work order, scoped to one repo

terms.meaning: built on the standard computer-science term "layered architecture" — each agent knows only its own layer and task, not the whole system. Is NOT: every agent reading every rule Example: `P7-DOCTRINE/layered-agents.md`, L0–L5 map
terms.is_not: every agent reading every rule Example: `P7-DOCTRINE/layered-agents.md`, L0–L5 map
terms.public_phrasing: "each part of the system only knows its own job"

terms.meaning: standard security-industry term "need-to-know" / "least privilege" — an agent gets only the minimum data and access needed for its task. Is NOT: loading the entire library into every agent Example: an L3 worker never receives locked-definitions, only its own task spec
terms.is_not: loading the entire library into every agent Example: an L3 worker never receives locked-definitions, only its own task spec
terms.example: an L3 worker never receives locked-definitions, only its own task spec
terms.public_phrasing: "need-to-know access" `LEGACY_ALIAS`: "least-knowledge" survives only inside the locked filename `AGENT_LAYERED_LAW_AND_LEAST_KNOWLEDGE_SSOT_LOCKED_v1.md` — new writing should say need-to-know / least privilege.

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster + standard software term ("code library") — an approved, organized collection. Is NOT: a random notes folder Example: `noetfield-library`
terms.is_not: a random notes folder Example: `noetfield-library`

terms.meaning: built on the standard business term "production line" — one full-stack instance (plan → produce → verify → ship → measure), replicated per revenue vertical. Is NOT: the same thing as "Lane" (a repo workstream — different concept, cross-referenced) Example: distribution line, factory line, TrustField line
terms.is_not: the same thing as "Lane" (a repo workstream — different concept, cross-referenced) Example: distribution line, factory line, TrustField line
terms.example: distribution line, factory line, TrustField line

terms.meaning: plain-English combination — a signal read directly from the running system right now, not from a file. Is NOT: a status a file claims Example: motor/loop health check reads receipts newer than twice the loop interval
terms.is_not: a status a file claims Example: motor/loop health check reads receipts newer than twice the loop interval
terms.example: motor/loop health check reads receipts newer than twice the loop interval

terms.meaning: our own file of approved definitions the brain is allowed to use — a small subset of the full dictionary. Is NOT: the full dictionary loaded into every worker Example: `P6-BRAIN-MEANING/locked-definitions-v1.md`
terms.is_not: the full dictionary loaded into every worker Example: `P6-BRAIN-MEANING/locked-definitions-v1.md`

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster + standard software term ("control loop") — a recurring task on a schedule, with a cost cap, a kill path, and a receipt per run. Is NOT: unbounded "always-on" · a Cursor session left open Example: terminology v1 §1
terms.is_not: unbounded "always-on" · a Cursor session left open Example: terminology v1 §1

terms.meaning: a safety rule only counts if a machine enforces it (a hook, a CI check, code) — not if it's just written instructions an agent is asked to follow. In our system: the test is "can an agent satisfy this by producing text, without the real condition being true?" If yes, it's prose and must be rewritten as code. Is NOT: "we documented the rule clearly" · a policy an agent is trusted to self-apply Example: `P7-DOCTRINE/mechanical-not-prose.md` — the `.agent-policy/` directory is hook-rejected on every agent lane Doctrine source file: `mechanical-not-prose.md` Public-safe rewrite: "our safety rules are enforced by code, not just written down"
terms.in_our_system: the test is "can an agent satisfy this by producing text, without the real condition being true?" If yes, it's prose and must be rewritten as code. Is NOT: "we documented the rule clearly" · a policy an agent is trusted to self-apply Example: `P7-DOCTRINE/mechanical-not-prose.md` — the `.agent-policy/` directory is hook-rejected on every agent lane Doctrine source file: `mechanical-not-prose.md` Public-safe rewrite: "our safety rules are enforced by code, not just written down"
terms.is_not: "we documented the rule clearly" · a policy an agent is trusted to self-apply Example: `P7-DOCTRINE/mechanical-not-prose.md` — the `.agent-policy/` directory is hook-rejected on every agent lane Doctrine source file: `mechanical-not-prose.md` Public-safe rewrite: "our safety rules are enforced by code, not just written down"
terms.example: `P7-DOCTRINE/mechanical-not-prose.md` — the `.agent-policy/` directory is hook-rejected on every agent lane Doctrine source file: `mechanical-not-prose.md` Public-safe rewrite: "our safety rules are enforced by code, not just written down"
terms.public_phrasing: "our safety rules are enforced by code, not just written down"
terms.doctrine_source_file: `mechanical-not-prose.md` Public-safe rewrite: "our safety rules are enforced by code, not just written down"
terms.banned_surfaces: none — safe to say publicly in plain form

terms.meaning: standard software/git-review term — the permission level required before a change can be merged. Is NOT: one blanket permission for all changes Example: `P8-MACHINE-LOOPS/merge-authority-tiers-T0-T3.md` — T0 docs/tests merge by machine, T3 schema/gates require the founder
terms.is_not: one blanket permission for all changes Example: `P8-MACHINE-LOOPS/merge-authority-tiers-T0-T3.md` — T0 docs/tests merge by machine, T3 schema/gates require the founder
terms.example: `P8-MACHINE-LOOPS/merge-authority-tiers-T0-T3.md` — T0 docs/tests merge by machine, T3 schema/gates require the founder
terms.public_phrasing: "graduated approval, based on risk" `CODE_ALIAS`: the T0–T3 tier labels themselves are literal gate names in code.

terms.meaning: standard term — a scheduler (e.g. cron) or executor (e.g. a long-running process runner) that starts loops on a timer. Is NOT: the loop's own logic Example: Cloudflare cron, Railway executor
terms.is_not: the loop's own logic Example: Cloudflare cron, Railway executor
terms.public_phrasing: "scheduler" / "executor" `[NEEDS ARCHITECT]`: unconfirmed whether "motor" is a literal identifier in code/config outside this repo — if so it survives as `CODE_ALIAS`.  ---

terms.meaning: standard legal term — Non-Disclosure Agreement, a confidentiality contract signed before sharing sensitive information. Is NOT: a verbal understanding Example: terminology v1 §5
terms.is_not: a verbal understanding Example: terminology v1 §5

terms.meaning: built on the standard QA term "negative testing" (proving a system correctly rejects bad input) — a gate is only proven by showing it reject something it should reject, not by a description claiming it works. Is NOT: prose asserting a gate works · a gate that has only ever seen passing input Example: `P7-DOCTRINE/negative-proof.md` — a deliberately-failing test commit the gate must reject, captured as a receipt
terms.is_not: prose asserting a gate works · a gate that has only ever seen passing input Example: `P7-DOCTRINE/negative-proof.md` — a deliberately-failing test commit the gate must reject, captured as a receipt
terms.example: `P7-DOCTRINE/negative-proof.md` — a deliberately-failing test commit the gate must reject, captured as a receipt
terms.public_phrasing: "we prove our checks by testing what they correctly reject"  ---

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "observation" (a noted, factual account) + "record" — a timestamped status snapshot, not full proof of payment or outcome. Is NOT: R≥1 proof · a substitute for a receipt on revenue claims Example: a fleet health pass
terms.is_not: R≥1 proof · a substitute for a receipt on revenue claims Example: a fleet health pass

terms.meaning: our own name for the parent thesis — every repeatable pattern the system runs on: healing, improvement, commercial, cost, buyer-signal, workflow-failure, or any other repeatable world/system pattern. Is NOT: the same thing as any one of its child patterns (Self-heal, Auto-heal, Signal Factory, Anti-theater, Brain-as-a-service are children, not the whole) Example: `P9-PATTERN-FACTORY/pattern-factory-index.md`
terms.is_not: the same thing as any one of its child patterns (Self-heal, Auto-heal, Signal Factory, Anti-theater, Brain-as-a-service are children, not the whole) Example: `P9-PATTERN-FACTORY/pattern-factory-index.md`

terms.meaning: our own name for the fixed schema an LLM's output must follow before it can run — the only shape a plan is allowed to take. Is NOT: free-form model output Example: `P5-LINE-ENGINE/execution-contract-as-brain.md`
terms.is_not: free-form model output Example: `P5-LINE-ENGINE/execution-contract-as-brain.md`

terms.meaning: standard agentic-AI pipeline role — breaks a goal into an ordered set of steps. Is NOT: the component that executes the steps Example: understanding → planner → router → workers pipeline
terms.is_not: the component that executes the steps Example: understanding → planner → router → workers pipeline
terms.public_phrasing: "the step that breaks a goal into an ordered plan"  ---

terms.meaning: our own shorthand — the first receipt of real payment from an external user. Is NOT: an internal test transaction · founder self-pay Example: terminology v1 §2
terms.is_not: an internal test transaction · founder self-pay Example: terminology v1 §2

terms.meaning: standard distributed-systems term (the Kubernetes "reconciliation" pattern) — the one component that resolves conflicts and decides the final state. Is NOT: any component that merely touches state Example: terminology v1 §1
terms.is_not: any component that merely touches state Example: terminology v1 §1

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "repair" + "loop" — a recurring, scheduled fix cycle. Is NOT: a one-time manual fix Example: `P8-MACHINE-LOOPS/repair-loop.md`, canon §4
terms.is_not: a one-time manual fix Example: `P8-MACHINE-LOOPS/repair-loop.md`, canon §4

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "research" + "loop" — a recurring, scheduled research cycle. Is NOT: a one-off search Example: `P8-MACHINE-LOOPS/research-loop.md`, canon §6
terms.is_not: a one-off search Example: `P8-MACHINE-LOOPS/research-loop.md`, canon §6

terms.meaning: our own name for a producer role that gathers outside information (e.g. via Perplexity or computer-use) to feed a line. Is NOT: the AI Stations (specialist producer roles that create content, not gather it — a related but separate role) Example: `P5-LINE-ENGINE/LINE_ENGINE_ARCHITECTURE_v0.2_HARDENED.md` §5.1
terms.is_not: the AI Stations (specialist producer roles that create content, not gather it — a related but separate role) Example: `P5-LINE-ENGINE/LINE_ENGINE_ARCHITECTURE_v0.2_HARDENED.md` §5.1

terms.tag: `TOMBSTONE → replaced by "confidential commercial terms" / "undisclosed commercial figure"`
terms.meaning: a specific number or contract detail that is never disclosed publicly. Is NOT: a rough public estimate Example: terminology v1 §5
terms.is_not: a rough public estimate Example: terminology v1 §5
terms.public_phrasing: "confidential commercial terms" `INTERNAL_ONLY`, never public in any specific form.

terms.meaning: our own term modeled on the standard business metric "return on investment" — applied to thinking/work instead of money: is this the highest-value use of effort right now, or should it be removed. Is NOT: "we're always busy" as a justification Example: `P7-DOCTRINE/return-on-cognition.md`
terms.is_not: "we're always busy" as a justification Example: `P7-DOCTRINE/return-on-cognition.md`

terms.meaning: standard software term — a dispatcher that sends work to the right destination based on rules, not a decision-maker itself. Is NOT: "the brain" · a component that makes judgment calls Example: capability router + circuit breaker, reclassified explicitly as dispatcher not brain
terms.is_not: "the brain" · a component that makes judgment calls Example: capability router + circuit breaker, reclassified explicitly as dispatcher not brain
terms.example: capability router + circuit breaker, reclassified explicitly as dispatcher not brain

terms.meaning: standard business term "ROI" (return on investment) + "engine" (a processing system) — the component that ranks lines by real return. Is NOT: a vanity metrics dashboard Example: `P5-LINE-ENGINE/LINE_ENGINE_ARCHITECTURE_v0.2_HARDENED.md` §7
terms.is_not: a vanity metrics dashboard Example: `P5-LINE-ENGINE/LINE_ENGINE_ARCHITECTURE_v0.2_HARDENED.md` §7
terms.public_phrasing: "the system that ranks by real return"  ---

terms.meaning: our own name for the one approved file that stores the schedule every live cron job must match exactly. Is NOT: any config file that happens to have a schedule in it Example: `data/noos-24-7-loops-v1.json`
terms.is_not: any config file that happens to have a schedule in it Example: `data/noos-24-7-loops-v1.json`

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster — choosing carefully among options, working with only a few partners chosen on evidence. Is NOT: snobbery · an unprovable "elite" claim Example: terminology v1 §5
terms.is_not: snobbery · an unprovable "elite" claim Example: terminology v1 §5

terms.meaning: our own name for the Line Engine's own bounded repair loop — fixes a line automatically, within a fixed scope, requiring real signal before improving anything. Is NOT: the same thing as Auto-heal (that's the broader Pattern Factory hospital-policy pattern) · an unbounded ability to change anything Example: `P5-LINE-ENGINE/LINE_ENGINE_ARCHITECTURE_v0.2_HARDENED.md` §4.1
terms.is_not: the same thing as Auto-heal (that's the broader Pattern Factory hospital-policy pattern) · an unbounded ability to change anything Example: `P5-LINE-ENGINE/LINE_ENGINE_ARCHITECTURE_v0.2_HARDENED.md` §4.1

terms.meaning: our own name for the applied skill that triages any raw inbound message into a fixed decision report — classification, need, four scores, one next action, a receipt. Is NOT: the same thing as Pattern Factory (Signal Factory is one applied child pattern, not the parent thesis) Example: `P9-PATTERN-FACTORY/signal-factory-v1.md` — implemented as the installed signal-factory skill
terms.is_not: the same thing as Pattern Factory (Signal Factory is one applied child pattern, not the parent thesis) Example: `P9-PATTERN-FACTORY/signal-factory-v1.md` — implemented as the installed signal-factory skill
terms.example: `P9-PATTERN-FACTORY/signal-factory-v1.md` — implemented as the installed signal-factory skill

terms.meaning: our own metaphor — "soup" = probabilistic model output carrying its own invented meaning; "raw" = a base model with no competing definitions, running on our fixed rules. Is NOT: a claim that any model is bad Example: `P7-DOCTRINE/deterministic-brain-doctrine.md` D-1/D-2
terms.is_not: a claim that any model is bad Example: `P7-DOCTRINE/deterministic-brain-doctrine.md` D-1/D-2

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster — someone or something scoped to one narrow task. Is NOT: a general-purpose worker with no scope limit Example: AI Stations, each an adversarial specialist role
terms.is_not: a general-purpose worker with no scope limit Example: AI Stations, each an adversarial specialist role
terms.example: AI Stations, each an adversarial specialist role

terms.meaning: standard distributed-systems term — two live instances of a system both acting as the authoritative source at once. Is NOT: two backups, one of which is clearly inactive Example: terminology v1 §4
terms.is_not: two backups, one of which is clearly inactive Example: terminology v1 §4
terms.public_phrasing: "two systems disagreeing about which one is authoritative"

terms.meaning: standard data-governance term "single source of truth" — the one authoritative file or record when copies disagree. Is NOT: the file that happens to be open right now Example: `sina-governance-SSOT`
terms.is_not: the file that happens to be open right now Example: `sina-governance-SSOT`

terms.meaning: a confidential business arrangement broader than a simple revenue-share — covers any structured commercial term (not only revenue splits) that stays private between the parties. Is NOT: only a revenue-sharing agreement · public pricing Example: terminology v1 §5
terms.is_not: only a revenue-sharing agreement · public pricing Example: terminology v1 §5
terms.public_phrasing: not public-facing, ever, in specific form `[NEEDS ARCHITECT]`: exact boundary of "broader than revenue share" not yet spelled out in any doc — flagging for a sharper definition when one is written.

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster (an underlying layer) + standard tech usage ("compute substrate") — the shared safety floor every line stands on. Is NOT: a line itself · something a line can bypass Example: `P5-LINE-ENGINE/LINE_ENGINE_ARCHITECTURE_v0.1.md` §3
terms.is_not: a line itself · something a line can bypass Example: `P5-LINE-ENGINE/LINE_ENGINE_ARCHITECTURE_v0.1.md` §3

terms.meaning: our own name for the rule that the Hub can only trigger irreversible actions manually (a tap), never run them automatically. Is NOT: full automation of irreversible actions Example: `P5-LINE-ENGINE/LINE_ENGINE_ARCHITECTURE_v0.1.md` §5.2
terms.is_not: full automation of irreversible actions Example: `P5-LINE-ENGINE/LINE_ENGINE_ARCHITECTURE_v0.1.md` §5.2

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "target" (a goal) + standard project-management term "blocker" (something stopping work) — only real damage should stop work; smaller issues get improved next cycle, not used as an excuse to freeze. Is NOT: an excuse to ignore real problems Example: `P7-DOCTRINE/targets-vs-blockers.md`
terms.is_not: an excuse to ignore real problems Example: `P7-DOCTRINE/targets-vs-blockers.md`
terms.public_phrasing: "we keep moving on targets, stop only for real blockers"

terms.meaning: standard support-tiering pattern, applied to our own offers — Tier 1: AI Spend Leak Audit (diagnostic). Tier 2: Cost Policy Pack. Tier 3: Model Firewall Pilot (enforcement, receipt-gated). Is NOT: interchangeable — Tier 3 enforcement claims require a customer receipt, per policy rule 4 Example: `P10-PRODUCT-LAYERS/agentic-cost-governance-service.md`
terms.is_not: interchangeable — Tier 3 enforcement claims require a customer receipt, per policy rule 4 Example: `P10-PRODUCT-LAYERS/agentic-cost-governance-service.md`

terms.meaning: standard distributed-database term (Cassandra and similar systems mark deleted rows with a "tombstone") — a marker that flags an item as dead so it's never used again. Is NOT: simply deleting a file with no record Example: terminology v1 §4
terms.is_not: simply deleting a file with no record Example: terminology v1 §4

terms.meaning: the name of a separate venture, currently reserved but not legally filed as a company. Is NOT: "TrustField Inc." · a Noetfield product or subsidiary Example: `P10-PRODUCT-LAYERS/trustfield.md`
terms.is_not: "TrustField Inc." · a Noetfield product or subsidiary Example: `P10-PRODUCT-LAYERS/trustfield.md`

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "validate" + "loop" — a recurring, scheduled correctness check. Is NOT: a one-time test run Example: `P8-MACHINE-LOOPS/validation-loop.md`, canon §2
terms.is_not: a one-time test run Example: `P8-MACHINE-LOOPS/validation-loop.md`, canon §2

terms.meaning: standard business/tech term — using no default AI provider, routing work by receipts, cancelable at any time. Is NOT: "agnostic" in customer-facing text · a refusal to ever partner Example: terminology v1 §2
terms.is_not: "agnostic" in customer-facing text · a refusal to ever partner Example: terminology v1 §2

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster "verify" + standard agentic-AI pipeline role — an independent component that checks correctness, separate from the one that produced the work. Is NOT: the producer checking itself Example: substrate's independent verifier
terms.is_not: the producer checking itself Example: substrate's independent verifier

terms.meaning: Merriam-Webster — one who performs a task. Here: an LLM or coding agent that drafts or runs bounded work, without deciding strategy. Is NOT: a decision-maker · the architect Example: terminology v1 §3
terms.is_not: a decision-maker · the architect Example: terminology v1 §3

terms.meaning: our own name for keeping the language of the system stable over time — connects directly to Drift, applied specifically to what words mean instead of what code runs. Is NOT: the same as system-health drift (Auto-heal handles that) — this is about language specifically Example: `P9-PATTERN-FACTORY/zero-drift-target.md`
terms.is_not: the same as system-health drift (Auto-heal handles that) — this is about language specifically Example: `P9-PATTERN-FACTORY/zero-drift-target.md`
terms.public_phrasing: "we keep our language consistent over time" Related: child pattern under Pattern Factory.

terms.meaning: our own term — the system proves its own state with receipts, without needing the founder to manually confirm it. Is NOT: the founder being cut out of irreversible decisions — those still require the founder Example: `P1-CANON/MACHINE_LOOPS_v1.md`
terms.is_not: the founder being cut out of irreversible decisions — those still require the founder Example: `P1-CANON/MACHINE_LOOPS_v1.md`
terms.public_phrasing: not public-facing (internal operating principle)  ---  ## Exceptions requiring a decision (not decided here)  1. **Motor** — recommend replacing with Scheduler/Executor. Cannot confirm from this repo whether "motor" is a literal code/filename identifier elsewhere (SourceA / Line Engine repos not mounted here). If it is, it survives as `CODE_ALIAS` instead of fully retiring. 2. **Structured commercial participation** — founder ruling says "broader than revenue share," but no document yet states the actual boundary. Meaning as written is best-effort; needs a sharper source before it's fully locked.  Everything else above is sourced and ready to lock as-is, or flag back individually.
